keySpecs: Qualcomm Snapdragon XR2 Gen 2 SoC, 8GB RAM
1832 x 1920 per-eye LCD resolution, fresnel lenses
Full-color passthrough mixed reality (4MP RGB cameras, no depth sensor)
128GB ($299.99) or 256GB ($399.99) storage, Touch Plus controllers included
Standalone all-in-one, ~2.2 hr battery; PCVR-capable via Link/Air Link
whoFor: Budget-minded newcomers who want standalone mixed reality plus wireless PCVR flight sim on a wallet-friendly headset.
imageNotes: Meta's own product page and Best Buy/Amazon listings are JS-rendered with no static og:image, and the Meta fbcdn image URL 403s on hotlink. Using the Wikimedia Commons product photo (verified HTTP 200, image/jpeg, 184KB) as a stable, hotlinkable direct asset.

keySpecs: QLED panels up to 3840 x 3840 per eye, 57 PPD 'retina-level' clarity
Glass aspheric lenses with local dimming; swappable optical engine (upgrade/repair)
Inside-out SLAM tracking, four cameras, no base stations required
120Hz eye tracking driving Dynamic Foveated Rendering
PCVR-only via DisplayPort; min RTX 2080, recommended RTX 3070+
whoFor: Enthusiast PCVR flight simmers chasing maximum cockpit-gauge sharpness and long-range spotting on high-end GPUs.
imageNotes: Clean white product shot pulled from the official Pimax Shopify product gallery (verified HTTP 200). The page og:image is a 'Mid-year sale' promo banner, so used the dedicated product-photo asset instead.

keySpecs: Dual 1-inch micro-OLED displays, 2560 x 2560 per eye
Ultralight 107g custom-molded body; 90Hz refresh
116 degree diagonal / 108 degree FoV with edge-to-edge clarity
Adjustable IPD mechanism (tool-set, mm markings)
SteamVR-tracked PCVR (1.0/2.0 base stations); 2e variant adds eye tracking + DFR
whoFor: Simmers who prize a featherweight, ultra-sharp OLED headset for long comfortable flight sessions and don't mind the SteamVR base-station setup.
imageNotes: Official Bigscreen Shopify og:image (product with strap), verified HTTP 200. Direct CDN asset from store.bigscreenvr.com.

keySpecs: 57-inch 7680 x 2160 Dual UHD (32:9) 1000R curved VA panel
240Hz refresh, 1ms response, DisplayPort 2.1 (needed for full res/refresh)
Quantum Mini-LED FALD, ~2392 dimming zones, DisplayHDR 1000, 1000-nit peak
AMD FreeSync Premium Pro, VRR support
Massive single-panel triple-monitor replacement for sim cockpits
whoFor: Sim racers who want a single super-ultrawide panel delivering triple-monitor width without bezels for maximum immersion.
imageNotes: Samsung Scene7 gallery asset for SKU LS57CG952NNXZA (verified HTTP 200). URL uses Samsung's $product-details-jpg$ preset image renderer; serves a clean product image.

keySpecs: Holds three screens from 32 to 65 inches for ~195 degrees peripheral vision
Fully free-standing (no desk/cockpit attachment required)
Lockable caster wheels for mobility + height-adjustable feet
Universal VESA mounting range; wide screen-position adjustment
Designed for events, sim-racing centers, and large triple-screen setups
whoFor: Sim racers running big triple displays who need a standalone, rollable stand independent of their cockpit or desk.
imageNotes: Direct product image from Next Level Racing's own cdn.nlr.sh (verified HTTP 200, image/png). One of six gallery assets on the official product page.

keySpecs: Blackwell architecture, 21,760 CUDA cores, ~2.41 GHz boost
32GB GDDR7, 512-bit bus, ~1.79 TB/s memory bandwidth
575W total graphics power
DLSS 4 with Multi Frame Generation
Flagship GPU for high-resolution PCVR flight sim (drives Crystal Super / triple 4K)
whoFor: No-compromise simmers driving retina-class PCVR headsets or triple-4K rigs at high frame rates.
imageNotes: Official NVIDIA og:image (Founders Edition card render), verified HTTP 200 image/jpeg from nvidia.com DAM. 1200x630 marketing asset.

keySpecs: Blackwell architecture (GB203), 10,752 CUDA cores, ~2.62 GHz boost
16GB GDDR7 memory
DLSS 4 with Multi Frame Generation
~360W class TGP, dual-slot Founders Edition design
High-end GPU for smooth PCVR and triple-monitor sim setups below flagship cost
whoFor: Simmers wanting near-flagship PCVR and triple-screen performance at a more reachable price than the 5090.
imageNotes: Official NVIDIA og:image (Founders Edition card render), verified HTTP 200 image/jpeg from nvidia.com DAM. 1200x630 marketing asset.
